title: centaurea jacea subsp. nigra
intro: Centaurea jacea subsp. nigra, also known as black knapweed, is a subspecies of the knapweed plant, a flowering perennial plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Centaurea jacea subsp. nigra include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant, protecting the body against free radicals. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
